gaps: No test case covers the specific scenario where denying location permission during onboarding causes a 'Data collection' dialog to repeatedly appear when tapping the device card on the Home/FMD screen, blocking navigation to Device Settings.
No test case validates the state transition from onboarding (with location denied) -> Home screen -> tapping device card -> expected Device Settings screen vs. actual Data collection dialog loop.
No test case covers the repeated/recurring nature of the Data collection dialog — tapping outside to dismiss and tapping again should navigate to Device Settings, not re-show the dialog.

No test case validates that the Data collection dialog's reappearance is tied specifically to the location permission denial during onboarding (as opposed to other permission denials or first-launch conditions).
No test case covers the interaction between the Find My Device (FMD) screen's device card tap action and the Data collection consent state when location permission was previously denied.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-001: Deny Location During Onboarding → Data Collection Dialog Blocks Device Settings Navigation
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / Find My Device (FMD) screen loads.
6. Locate the device card for the paired Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
7. Tap the device card once.
8. Observe the dialog that appears.
9. If a 'Data collection' dialog appears, tap outside the dialog (tap on the background/scrim) to dismiss it.
10. Immediately tap the same device card again.
11. Observe the dialog that appears this second time.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 7: A 'Data collection' dialog appears instead of navigating to Device Settings. Step 11: The 'Data collection' dialog reappears again — the user is stuck in a loop and cannot reach Device Settings. This confirms the bug where denying location permission during onboarding causes the Data collection dialog to block navigation repeatedly.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-002: Verify Data Collection Dialog Does NOT Reappear When Location Was Granted During Onboarding
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Allow' / 'While using the app'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
6. Locate the device card for the paired Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
7. Tap the device card once.
8. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: The app navigates directly to the Device Settings screen for the selected device. No 'Data collection' dialog appears. This confirms the dialog reappearance is specifically tied to location permission denial during onboarding, not a general first-launch condition.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-003: Deny Location During Onboarding → Dismiss Data Collection Dialog → Navigate Away and Back → Tap Device Card Again
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
6. Tap the device card — observe the 'Data collection' dialog appears.
7. Tap outside the dialog to dismiss it.
8. Navigate to a different tab/screen in the app (e.g., tap 'Settings' tab or 'Devices' tab).
9. Navigate back to the Home / FMD screen.
10. Tap the same device card again.
11. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 10: The 'Data collection' dialog reappears again, blocking navigation to Device Settings. The dialog is not a one-time occurrence — it persists across tab switches and screen navigation when location was denied during onboarding.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-004: Deny Location During Onboarding → Kill and Relaunch App → Tap Device Card → Verify Dialog Persists
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
6. Tap the device card — observe the 'Data collection' dialog appears.
7. Tap outside the dialog to dismiss it.
8. Fully kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps or force stop).
9. Relaunch the app.
10. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
11. Tap the device card.
12. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 11: The 'Data collection' dialog reappears after app restart. The dialog state is not reset by killing and relaunching the app — the bug persists across app lifecycle restarts when location was denied during onboarding.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-005: Deny Location During Onboarding → Accept Data Collection Dialog → Verify Device Settings Opens
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
6. Tap the device card — observe the 'Data collection' dialog appears.
7. Tap the 'Accept' / 'Agree' / positive action button on the Data collection dialog.
8. Observe the result.
9. Navigate away to another tab (e.g., Settings tab).
10. Navigate back to Home / FMD screen.
11. Tap the device card again.
12. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: The app navigates to the Device Settings screen for the selected device. Step 11: The app navigates directly to Device Settings again — no Data collection dialog reappears. This confirms that accepting the Data collection consent resolves the issue, but the dialog should not be blocking navigation in the first place when location was denied.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-006: Deny Location During Onboarding → Verify Other Permission Denials Do NOT Trigger Data Collection Dialog
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. When prompted for any other permissions (e.g., Notifications, Bluetooth, Nearby Devices if applicable), also tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
5. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
6. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads.
7. Tap the device card.
8. Observe the dialog that appears.
9. Dismiss the Data collection dialog by tapping outside.
10. Go to Android Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Permissions.
11. Grant Location permission ('Allow all the time' or 'While using the app').
12. Return to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
13. Tap the device card.
14. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: The 'Data collection' dialog appears (triggered by location denial). Step 13: After granting Location permission, the app navigates directly to Device Settings — no Data collection dialog appears. This confirms the dialog reappearance is specifically tied to the Location permission denial state, not other permission denials.

generated_testcases.title: TC-FMD-DC-007: Deny Location During Onboarding → Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect → Tap Device Card → Verify Dialog Persists
steps: 1. Fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app (or clear app data).
2. Launch the app and proceed through onboarding.
3. When prompted for Location permission during onboarding, tap 'Deny' / 'Don't Allow'.
4. Complete remaining onboarding steps.
5. Observe the Home / FMD screen loads with Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC connected.
6. Tap the device card — observe the 'Data collection' dialog appears.
7. Tap outside the dialog to dismiss it.
8. Disconnect the earbuds via Bluetooth settings or by powering off the charge case.
9. Wait 5 seconds, then reconnect the earbuds.
10. Return to the Poly Studio Mobile app Home / FMD screen.
11. Tap the device card.
12. Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 11: The 'Data collection' dialog reappears after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle. The dialog state is not reset by Bluetooth state transitions — the bug persists across reconnection when location was denied during onboarding.
